steps.title: Identify the defining contrast in Text 2
steps.content: Text 2 establishes that research has wrongly focused on short-term merger effects (which harm consumers) while overlooking long-term effects (several years or more), where efficiency gains create consumer benefits.

steps.title: Apply the time-frame contrast to Lin's findings
steps.content:
Lin found that prices would rise—a short-term, consumer-unfavorable outcome that exemplifies the kind of effect Rinaldi and Costa critique.

They would urge her to investigate whether this effect persists over the They would encourage Lin to investigate whether the projected effect on subscription prices persists over an extended period. is correct.
